business_name: Sought After Aircraft Fuel & Service Center
industry: Aerospace — aviation services / aircraft maintenance / FBO
financials_note: Disclosed Sales ($4,112,992) and Cash Flow ($123,948) cover only the 10 months ending October 2023. Listing first posted Nov 8 2023, renewed Nov 1 2024. Figures are stale by ~2.5 years as of evaluation date and must be re-requested from the broker as full-year, current-period statements.

broker.name: Michael Floorman
broker.title: President
broker.firm_note: Largest business brokerage in the Western US; 5,600+ closed transactions since 1981

business_detail: Fully staffed aircraft service center and airport operation. Aviation mechanical services; manages aircraft hangar and office rentals; aircraft sales and brokerage; fuel services and sales; vehicle rental. Ground lease permits ground and flight school, aircraft maintenance and repair, parts sales, charter and rental, avionics upgrades, and aircraft management. Management will stay post-sale.
ebitda: needs broker follow-up — disclosed 10-mo cash flow $123,948 (~$148.7K annualized est.); separate EBITDA not disclosed and is very likely sub-$1M
ebitda_margin_note: Disclosed cash flow $123,948 / disclosed sales $4,112,992 = 3.0% (cash-flow margin, same 10-mo period). True EBITDA margin needs broker follow-up.
flags: STALE FINANCIALS — disclosed figures cover only the 10 months ending Oct 2023; ~2.5 years old as of 2026-05-21
EBITDA far below Buy Box floor — disclosed 10-mo cash flow $123,948, annualizes to ~$148.7K, vs. $1M minimum
Thin margin — ~3.0% cash-flow margin vs. 15% target (fuel resale is structurally low-margin)
Asking price undisclosed — valuation multiple cannot be computed
Ground-lease dependency — business operates on a leasehold, not owned land; lease terms, remaining years, and renewal risk are unknown and could be a deal-killer
